description: Queue of section expansion tasks for the analyst. Each item references a curmudgeon review that found major weaknesses. The analyst writes complete replacement text, one item per run.

items.target: KILLSHOT-GAIA section (Part 4.3 in generate-html.js, or sections.json after extraction)
items.notes: Expanded KILLSHOT-GAIA section. Content verified in data/sections.json part4. Superseded by stronger EXP-006 (which is also in part4). Both share same heading — EXP-006 tail content confirmed present. Integration retroactively confirmed 2026-04-07.

items.target: KILLSHOT-GPS section (Part 4.2 in generate-html.js, or sections.json after extraction)
items.notes: Expanded KILLSHOT-GPS section. Content verified in data/sections.json part4. Integration retroactively confirmed 2026-04-07.
Expanded SEC-4.5.9 V13 coordinate system. Content verified in data/sections.json part4b. Integration retroactively confirmed 2026-04-07.

items.target: Section 3.1 (Live Power Dashboard) and Section 3.2 (Eclipse Kill-Shot) in data/sections.json part3
items.notes: Targeted patches (not full rewrite). SEC-3.2 was already substantially rewritten between curmudgeon Cycle 1 and now. 4 patches: P1=Domain#20 stale range, P2=Polaris bottom-line inconsistency, P3+P4=KAPPA/FSF conflation in two locations. ISS-082/083 mostly already fixed in current text. ISS-074 (archive Live Power page) deferred to poller. Note: EXP-004.json contains SEC-4.8 content from previous run (ISS-418/419); actual SEC-3.1/3.2 expansion is in EXP-004-sec31-32.json.

items.target: Section 6.2 (Eclipse Test section) in data/sections.json part6
items.notes: SEC-6.2 rewritten. Key changes: (1) Dual-baseline structure accurately described (-5 to -26 nT combined). (2) monitor.py computation verified: -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ -28.9 nT (corrected from -29.1). (3) FSF discrepancy documented: monitor.py uses KAPPA (1.672), predictions page uses per-station FSFs (0.642-2.075), inject_ai_layer.py uses latitude_factor — three different formulas. (4) E-PRED tests acknowledged, E-PRED-B flagged as strongest sub-test. (5) Kp qualifier added. (6) W004 provenance and third-attempt pattern included. Note: EXP-005.json contains misrouted EXP-008 taxonomy content; actual SEC-6.2 expansion is in EXP-005-sec62.json.

items.target: WIN-002 detail_evidence in data/wins.json + WIN-048 detail_evidence + detail_verdict_text
items.notes: WIN-002: Complementarity defense already addressed in prior patches; only fix needed is removing stale 'forthcoming section expansion' placeholder sentence. WIN-048: Full rewrite — de-duplicated triple-repeated paragraph, added ΛCDM counter-prediction framing, ecliptic alignment argument, look-elsewhere effect, specific DOI citations (Planck 2015 XXIII, Land & Magueijo 2005), Planck 2018 confirmation. Both kernels of truth acknowledged. Note: EXP-006.json contains old KILLSHOT-GAIA content; actual WIN-002/WIN-048 expansion is in EXP-006-win002-win048.json.

items.target: WIN-024, WIN-031, WIN-032 detail_evidence in data/wins.json
items.notes: WIN-024: Cleaned duplicated paragraphs (longitude mismatch + code evidence each appeared twice), removed duplicated Cnossen 2016 note from claim field, moved coupling research acknowledgment to detail_extra, clarified SAA boundary definition to standard 32,000 nT reference. WIN-031: Expanded from 130 to 280 words — added Eliade axis mundi cross-cultural evidence (Hindu/Norse/Greek/Islamic/Zoroastrian), retrodiction vs prediction distinction, non-discriminating convergence argument, monitor.py absence. WIN-032: Expanded and differentiated from WIN-031 — named category error (future supernatural event as confirmed prediction), added Revelation 21:2 exegesis (pole-axis is dome author's addition), cited geopolitical framing from context.html, quantified scorecard inflation (4 theological WINs), distinguished from WIN-031 (no geometric core).

items.target: Verdict taxonomy review — data/wins.json verdict fields + data/sections.json verdict legend
items.notes: ISS-442: Added subcategory-clarifying paragraph to section 2.6 distinguishing 4 failure modes (data misrepresentation, count inflation, curve-fitting, non-discriminating). ISS-443: Cleaned WIN-043/060 verdict text to remove duplication language, replaced with non-discrimination framing matching Std Model Explains verdict. ISS-445: Added tidal grouping acknowledgment to section 2.3. Also proposed Evaluation Guide addition addressing missing positive verdict category. No verdict reclassifications — taxonomy preserved as 6 categories. Note: EXP-008.json contains old misrouted content; actual taxonomy expansion is in EXP-008-taxonomy.json.

items.target: Section 3.5.4 (Globe Values Relabeled) — WIN-024 structural inconsistency
items.notes: WIN-024 (Roaring 40s / SAA boundary) is placed in SEC-3.5.4 (relabeling of standard physics) but WIN-024 has relabels_standard=false. The claim is about a spurious correlation (geographic coincidence interpreted as causation), not vocabulary relabeling. The paragraph needs to either be moved to a different section or the section framing needs to distinguish the two error types. Requires prose restructuring of 3.5.4. COMPLETED: Removed WIN-024 paragraph (spurious correlation, not relabeling). Added bridging paragraph naming WIN-024 with cross-reference to detail table. Preserved 3 genuine relabeling examples. Fixed stale SAA latitude range. Output in EXP-009-sec354.json.

items.target: Section 4.8 (Solar Angular Diameter Kill-Shot) in data/sections.json
items.notes: ISS-418: 50% vs 30% numerical inconsistency in our own solar size argument. ISS-419: Missing aetheric refraction defense (dome AI Context Rule 11). Full rewrite needed.
items.integration_note: Analyst confirmed sections.json already contains all required content (ISS-418 50% fix and ISS-419 refraction defense both present). No replacement_html needed.

items.notes: ISS-453: Tab swap to put Self-Contradictions (selftest) before External Tests (falsify). Key finding: sections.json prose was ALREADY updated to assume this reorder ('above', 'come first') but generate-html.js tab order was never changed — creating a prose/structure inconsistency. Expansion specifies 6 edits to generate-html.js: tab bar buttons, TOC order, 3 sectionNav chain links, div order. No content changes needed. All ~20 showTab() cross-references unaffected (use tab IDs which don't change). Both sides considered: self-contradictions are categorically stronger (dome-parameter-only, no external data to dispute), but Part numbering (4.5 before 4) is a minor visual inconsistency — mitigated by descriptive tab labels.

items.target: WIN-012 detail_evidence in data/wins.json — spatial heterogeneity loophole + two-eclipse counterargument
items.notes: Full rewrite. De-duplicated two repeated paragraphs. ISS-467: spatial heterogeneity closed (three-count: no advance specification, κ not universal if heterogeneous, 7 eclipses with zero replication). ISS-468: two-eclipse rhetoric addressed (BOU=magnetic only, Mohe=gravity only, ratio is retrospective). B(r) 62376/64852 corrected. Bessel vs exponential. OPEN-011 cited. Includes replacement_detail_verdict_text.

items.target: WIN-015 detail_evidence in data/wins.json — Meyl scalar wave analysis expansion
items.notes: Full expansion addressing all Cycle 1 + Cycle 2 holes. Bruhn 2006 (4 mathematical errors), Kuhlke 2010, near-field coupling mechanism with λ/2π criterion, precision experiment argument (CUORE+KATRIN+LIGO), dome code analysis (bare metadata + domain 8 mislabeling), theory vs experiment distinction, retracted paper reframed. Dome claim fetched and quoted. Replacement covers detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and finding fields.
items.integration_notes: Applied replacement_fields from EXP-013-win015-meyl.json to WIN-015 (detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, finding). Meyl scalar wave analysis expanded from 568 to 3280 chars.

items.target: Raw-text archive of dome Live Power page + SEC-3.1 verifiability note
items.notes: Live Power page (live.html) archived to raw-text/08-live-power.txt — captures static HTML structure (20 domain descriptions, formulas, falsification thresholds, correlation matrix, AI Auditor checklist) and dynamic data snapshot from status_history.json (2026-04-05T04:40:56Z). SEC-3.1 verifiability footnote written as recommended patch in EXP-014 output. Additional findings: σ discrepancy (9.2σ static vs 14.26σ dynamic), pred=obs identity pattern (3+ domains), NMP 50% adaptive tolerance, 6/20 domains unscored, unfalsifiable thresholds. Kernel of truth acknowledged: infrastructure is genuinely transparent and auditable.
items.integration_notes: Applied SEC-3.1 verifiability footnote to data/sections.json part3, after 'There is no out-of-sample prediction.' paragraph. Archive file was already created by analyst at raw-text/08-live-power.txt.

items.target: WIN-015 detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text in data/wins.json — Meyl scalar wave (revised version, longer)
items.notes: Analyst wrote a second, longer (3765 chars) version of WIN-015 detail_evidence. EXP-013 (3280 chars) was applied in this run. This may be a further revision — human should compare against the applied version and decide whether to upgrade.

items.integration_notes: Content was already present in data/sections.json part4b at idx 1337. EXP-016 recorded existing state. No changes needed.
items.notes: Analyst documented the current Section 4.5.1 Schumann content. Content already matches EXP-016 replacement_html.

items.target: Eclipse kill-shot section in data/sections.json (part6 or wherever eclipse section lives) — E-PRED-B argument
items.notes: SEC-3.2 eclipse section completely ignores E-PRED-B (Hartland > Ebro differential prediction — dome predicts larger signal at stations with greater coverage reduction, globe does not). This is the dome's strongest pre-registered prediction (geometric/relative, independent of absolute baseline), and our section does not address it at all. Analyst should: (1) describe E-PRED-B formally, (2) explain why it is the dome's strongest test, (3) assess what the current evidence shows about Hartland vs Ebro differential, (4) write replacement prose that acknowledges E-PRED-B and explains why it remains incomplete pending August 2026 eclipse data. — SUPERSEDED: ISS-083 was pre-expansion. E-PRED-B is already thoroughly addressed in data/sections.json part3. No action needed.

items.target: Archive dome Python source files (inject_ai_layer.py, update_optical_caveats.py, monitor.py, recalc_v51.py) from GitHub repo and update Parts 5.14/5.16 claims
items.notes: ISS-423: Sections 5.14 and 5.16 make specific claims about Python scripts. These files should be fetched from the dome repo (john09289/predictions on GitHub), archived in our project as evidence, and the prose updated to reference specific archived versions. Also resolve ISS-429 residual concern: verify what the actual eclipse prediction code does in monitor.py and replace the hedged claim with a specific source-code citation.
